conclusion: Replay artifacts are internally trustworthy, but trace/lifecycle semantic reconciliation remains required before large-scale research.
findings: Historical lifecycle timestamp failure was found in prior artifacts; corrected artifacts pass.

timestamp_audit.remediation: Set the lifecycle clock to the current candle timestamp before creating each entry lifecycle. Re-run the production replay and deterministic verification, and retire or relabel artifacts generated before the correction.
timestamp_audit.root_cause: Before the correction, replayRunner initialized lifecycle metadata time from replay_timestamp. Entry lifecycle events therefore used the replay wall-clock timestamp, while historical exit events used candle timestamps. The first historical event appeared after its later exit, so lifecycle ordering failed.

classifications.explanation: Lifecycle records paper proposals while the trace records actionable stage outcomes.
Lifecycle does not separately emit this decision stage.

verification.production_claim: Production replay artifacts were generated and matched across all deterministic replays.
